body:
## Sync service

First cut of `services/sync` — keeps a user's notes consistent across devices.

### What's here
- WebSocket endpoint `/sync`, authenticated via JWT (`?token=`)
- Per-user `ConnectionRegistry` with presence tracking
- Broadcasts `SyncEvent`s to a user's other connected devices
- `POST /events` so notes-api can push change events for fan-out
- Last-write-wins reconciliation keyed on the note `version` (`shouldApply`)
- `GET /health` exposing `SYNC_PROTOCOL_VERSION`
- `node:test` coverage for reconciliation

### Follow-ups (out of scope here)
- Durable change log + replay on reconnect (offline catch-up)
- Move from last-write-wins to CRDT/OT for field-level merges
- Horizontal scaling (Redis pub/sub) so fan-out works across instances
- Auth via the auth `/verify` endpoint or JWKS instead of a shared secret

Closes #3
title: feat(sync): real-time note sync across devices

body:
## Notes CRUD API

First cut of `services/notes-api` — owns note persistence.

### What's here
- `POST /notes`, `GET /notes`, `GET /notes/:id`, `PATCH /notes/:id`, `DELETE /notes/:id`
- JWT auth middleware (validates tokens issued by the auth service)
- Ownership enforcement — a user only sees/edits their own notes (404 otherwise)
- In-memory `NoteStore`; `version` + `updatedAt` bumped on every write
- Emits a `SyncEvent` on every mutation (logged for now) so the sync service can consume it
- `node:test` coverage for the store

### Follow-ups (out of scope here)
- Real datastore (Postgres) behind `NoteStore`
- Wire `emitSyncEvent` to the sync service (HTTP/bus) instead of logging
- Pagination + full-text search on list
- Validate tokens via auth `/verify` or JWKS instead of a shared secret

Closes #2
title: feat(notes-api): CRUD endpoints for notes

body:
## Auth service

First cut of `services/auth` — the identity source of truth.

### What's here
- `POST /register` — email + password (bcrypt, 8-char min), returns user + JWT
- `POST /login` — validates credentials, returns a signed JWT
- `POST /verify` — lets notes-api / sync validate tokens
- `GET /health`
- In-memory user store behind a swappable interface (DB later)
- Token issue/verify built on the shared `AuthTokenClaims` type
- `node:test` round-trip test for token issue/verify

### Follow-ups (intentionally out of scope here)
- Real datastore (Postgres) behind `UserStore`
- Refresh tokens / rotation
- Rate limiting on login

Closes #1
title: feat(auth): user registration, login, and JWT issuance
